resources.title: The SaSMaster Knowledge Graph
resources.subtitle: graphify × Obsidian corpus map · nightly refresh · D3 force · Leiden communities
resources.description: Interactive map of the platform's own mind: every skill, schema, card, agent and decision as one connected corpus. Embeds the degree≥2 signal subgraph of the nightly graphify build (all header counts derive from the loaded data, never typed); drop the full graph.json onto the page for the complete corpus. Light edition is the analysis tool; the cinematic edition (Vanta/GSAP/Lenis) is the deep/demo view with the 22-domain taxonomy legend.

resources.title: Agentic OS — Executive Brief
resources.subtitle: JARVIS becomes the platform's operating system · light edition · 2026-07-16

resources.title: BRAIN-001 — The Claude Context Brain
resources.subtitle: L1 reflex · L2 warm · L3 live (MotherDuck ops.platform_state) · seeded 2026-07-13 · 12 state keys · 138-skill L1 reflex layer · any Claude, anywhere
resources.description: The distributed context brain that makes every Claude session (Mac, cloud, mobile Cowork, JARVIS/Slack) rehydrate from one truth. Splits context by rate of change: L1 Reflex — 138 skill descriptions always in context (~free) · L2 Warm — sasmaster-context hub skill (~2k tokens on trigger) · L3 Live — single-copy MotherDuck sasmaster.ops.platform_state, one SQL query, never stale, append-only history in ops.platform_state_log. Writers: local close-out ritual (verified=true), JARVIS !logstate (verified=false until re-checked), cron watermarks. Precedence: owner skill beats hub on depth; L3 beats everything on state. Includes distributed-memory map (working/semantic/state/episodic/credential) and the live-seeded L3 state table. Spine figure corrected same-day to 12,955,801 is_current per Lessons ledger, mistake preserved in platform_state_log.

resources.title: EIDR Parallel Harvest Engine — Technology Spotlight
resources.subtitle: FUSION-EIDR-ALTID · 306K EIDR records · 75 AltIDs/title · EC2 parallel fleet · Claude-orchestrated · Jun 2026
resources.description: AI-orchestrated, cloud-parallelized pipeline that captures EIDR content metadata and up to 75 Alternate IDs per title — then fuses them straight into the SaSMaster Parent Key spine. Claude acts as the engineering subagent; AWS EC2 provides the horizontal parallelism (true parallel virtual displays) a single workstation could never deliver. Covers L1 bulk metadata REST lane and L3 browser-extracted AltID lane.

resources.title: SaSMaster Brand Reference v8
resources.subtitle: Canonical design system · 5 themes · 46 chart types · 209 reporting catalog entries · 7 families
resources.description: Canonical design system — 5 themes, 46 chart types, 209-entry reporting catalog across 7 families (Programming, Marketing, Advertising, Rights & Royalties, Cross Platform, Social Analytics, Digital Analytics / Other). Includes Evan Shapiro / ESHAP media intelligence framework.

resources.title: Schema v2.0 Migration — Executive Brief
resources.subtitle: 12.9M spine rows · 332K CERTIFIED staged · 25.5M source rows · ZERO data loss · 2026-06-20
resources.description: Solo-founder executive brief on the Schema v2.0 migration. 11 task cards, 04:00–07:45 AM ET build session. Five-table spine (parent_key · pk_gn_xref · nielsen_telecast · alt_ids · coverage_summary), dual surrogate key pairs (pk_sk BIGINT + SASMASTER_PKID UUIDv5, frozen namespace 7b7b4ce5), EIDR-gated CERTIFIED band (eidr_id NOT NULL + score ≥ 0.90), tall Alt-ID catalog (172+ domains, 344K Wikidata IDs), person-prefix collision prevention. v1 cold-archived with parity PASS before any DROP. One command from CERTIFIED: python3 scripts/eidr/apply_eidr_backfill.py --live.

resources.title: SaSMaster ASK — Audience Signal Intelligence
resources.subtitle: NL Query Interface · Audience Signals · Data Lake Access · Draft Design · 2026-06-11
resources.description: Draft design and experience for SaSMaster ASK — the natural language query interface for audience signal intelligence. Covers trend, price, supply, identity, social, and audience signal dimensions with interactive chip-driven queries against the SaSMaster data lake.

resources.title: Data Orchestration & Intelligent Matching
resources.subtitle: Two-tier orchestration · Parent ID & CID · End-to-end flow · Match & Mint · Curated layers · Audit · 2026-06-11
resources.description: Executive visualization of the SaSMaster data orchestration and intelligent matching system. Covers the two-tier orchestration layer (source fidelity vs certified consumption), SASMASTER ID generation (Parent ID & CID minted once at write time), end-to-end flow from ingestion through lake → delta → match & mint → curated layers → audit.

resources.title: Curated Metadata Layer
resources.subtitle: Tier 2 · Stage 5 downstream of Match · Mint · Certify · Band-based admission · EIDR gate · 2026-06-11
resources.description: Task card for the SaSMaster Curated Metadata Layer. Covers where it sits (Tier 2 of the orchestration layer, Stage 5 downstream of Match → Mint → Certify), the curated build flow (cert run lands → agent wakes → gate → enrich → QA → serve, idempotent + cron-safe), and band-based admission policy (v2 confidence model, certified is EIDR-gated, consumers choose rigor).

resources.title: Data Orchestration · Matching & Certification

resources.title: The Self-Learning Flywheel
resources.subtitle: Rim: QUESTION IN → COMPOSE → SERVE → BLESS → DEPOSIT → PROMOTE · Control Plane at hub · DEPOSIT-GATE-001
resources.description: Canonical model of the SaSMaster self-learning loop. Six work stages form the rim; the control plane sits at the hub, governing every stage and metering autonomy. DEPOSIT (stage 5) is enforced by DEPOSIT-GATE-001 — no product slice reaches DONE without a certified catalog entry.

resources.title: The Control Plane, Explained
resources.subtitle: What it is · §15 gate · why it fails silently · JARVIS-QUEUE-001 case · 2026-06-07

Covers the air-traffic-tower analogy, 2-layer system diagram (Control Plane vs Workload), IS/IS-NOT classification guide, why silent failure makes it special, the full 6-step §15 gate, and a 5-second dispatch test. Written post JARVIS-QUEUE-001 incident (Jun 7 2026): one hardcoded-credential line stalled the queue ~5.5hrs with zero errors.

resources.title: Research Portal v2 · RAG+CAG Intelligence
resources.subtitle: DuckDB BM25 KB search · CAG title briefs · 29 methodology entries · Dr. Scoop wired
resources.description: RESEARCH-PORTAL-002: RAG+CAG intelligence layer for Dr. Scoop. KB files (PRIM/NARR/CLAIM/MOAT/MISSION — 29 entries) committed to command-api/knowledge/. DuckDB in-memory BM25 index (kb-search.ts). CAG title brief engine with SSE streaming (/api/scoop/brief/:tmdbId). Every chat request injects top-3 KB entries via cache_control ephemeral. Portal _sendTitleBrief() calls CAG endpoint first, falls back to generic streamScoop(). Drill-down P3.5/P4: CARD_META enrichment (20 titles), switchDrillTab(), _buildRightsGrid(), openTrailer(), cast monogram fix. Built 2026-05-26.

resources.title: The Genre Inversion — Supply-Side Evidence for Comedy Arbitrage
resources.caption: SaSMaster spine analysis: comedy TV supply share 28%→15.9% (2015–2025); drama 2.3× multiplier; crossover 2021. 861K parent keys, DuckDB GENRE-SUPPLY-001. Backs 'Media Freakonomics: The Genre Arbitrage'.

resources.title: Theme 05 — Animated Handsketch
resources.subtitle: Three-mode brand theme switcher · Blueprint · Sketch · Light · rough.js canvas
resources.description: SaSMaster UI/UX brand theme exploration v4. Three switchable themes: Blueprint (dark navy, Raleway light condensed), Sketch (warm parchment, Caveat/Patrick Hand), and Light (clean default). Animated hand-drawn canvas borders via rough.js on all card elements. Full nav, KPI strip, chart placeholders, and data table mocked in each theme mode. Serves as the canonical brand reference for portal skin decisions.

resources.title: Box Office Intelligence — UI/UX v4
resources.subtitle: Studio performance · Khroma palette · DM Serif Display · Inter · Full dashboard mock
resources.description: SaSMaster Box Office Intelligence portal UI/UX exploration v4. Khroma-curated 8-color palette (coral, electric periwinkle, warm gold, mint teal, dusty rose, sage, soft violet, butter yellow). Studio breakdown bars for Disney/Universal/Warner/Paramount/Sony. Weekly chart, genre heatmap, title performance table, and market share ring. Typography: DM Serif Display for headlines, Inter for data. Serves as the design reference for the BOXOFFICE-INTEL-001 portal surface.
